description: Process asynchronous jobs and events through a provider-agnostic message queue supporting publish, subscribe, acknowledge, retry with backoff, and dead-letter queues

validation.message: Queue name must be alphanumeric with dots, hyphens, or underscores (max 256 chars)

fields.name: message_id
fields.label: Unique message identifier assigned by the queue

fields.name: delay_seconds
fields.label: Delay before message becomes visible to consumers

validation.message: Delay must be at least 0 seconds

fields.name: retry_count
fields.label: Number of times this message has been retried

validation.message: Max retries must be at least 0

fields.name: dead_letter_queue
fields.label: Dead-letter queue name for failed messages

fields.name: visibility_timeout
fields.label: Seconds a message is hidden after being consumed (processing window)

validation.message: Visibility timeout must be at least 1 second
Visibility timeout must not exceed 43200 seconds (12 hours)

fields.name: group_id
fields.label: Message group for FIFO ordering guarantee

fields.name: deduplication_id
fields.label: Deduplication ID for exactly-once within dedup window

delivery: At-least-once delivery guarantee by default; consumers must be idempotent
FIFO ordering available when group_id is set; messages in same group processed in order
Exactly-once delivery available with deduplication_id (5-minute dedup window)
message_size: For larger payloads, store data in cloud storage and pass a reference in the message
retry: Failed messages are retried with exponential backoff: delay = base_delay * 2^retry_count
Default max_retries is 3; configurable per queue
Messages exceeding max_retries are moved to the dead-letter queue
Dead-letter queue messages retain original payload and all retry metadata
visibility: Visibility timeout must exceed expected processing time
If processing exceeds visibility timeout, message becomes visible to other consumers
Consumers must explicitly acknowledge (ack) successful processing
Negative acknowledgment (nack) returns message to queue immediately for retry
ordering: Standard queues provide best-effort ordering (no strict guarantee)
FIFO queues guarantee order within a message group_id
FIFO queues have lower throughput than standard queues

given: Queue exists and publisher has write permission
message_published.result: Message accepted by queue; message_id returned for tracking

given: Consumer polls queue and message is available
message_consumed.result: Message delivered to consumer; hidden from other consumers for visibility_timeout duration

message_acknowledged.result: Message permanently removed from queue; processing complete

given: Consumer sends negative acknowledgment or visibility timeout expires
message_retried.result: Message returned to queue with incremented retry count; exponential backoff delay applied

message_dead_lettered.result: Message moved to dead-letter queue with full retry history for investigation

given: Consumer polls queue but no messages are available
queue_empty.result: Empty response returned; consumer should back off with polling interval

message_too_large.result: Publish rejected; store large payloads externally and pass a reference

queue_not_found.result: Operation rejected; queue must be created before publishing or consuming

given: Message with same deduplication_id published within dedup window
duplicate_message.result: Duplicate detected; message not re-queued; original message_id returned

errors.message: Message payload exceeds 256KB limit. Store large data externally and pass a reference.
Queue does not exist. Create the queue before publishing or consuming messages.
Caller does not have permission to publish to or consume from this queue.
Message processing exceeded visibility timeout. Message returned to queue.
Dead-letter queue has reached its retention limit. Investigate and drain failed messages.

related.reason: Incoming webhooks can be queued for async processing
Email sends can be queued for reliable delivery
Large message payloads stored in cloud storage with queue reference
